records.ety: [AS. aweg, anweg, onweg; on on + weg way.]

quotes: Have me away, for I am sore wounded.

defs: Absent; gone; at a distance; as, the master is away from home.

quotes: The axis of rotation is inclined away from the sun.

defs: From a state or condition of being; out of existence.
quotes: Be near me when I fade away.

defs: By ellipsis of the verb, equivalent to an imperative: Go or come away; begone; take away.
quotes: And the Lord said . . . Away, get thee down.

defs: On; in continuance; without intermission or delay; as, sing away.
notes: ☞ It is much used in phrases signifying moving or going from; as, go away, run away, etc.; all signifying departure, or separation to a distance. Sometimes without the verb; as, whither away so fast ? “Love hath wings, and will away.” Waller. It serves to modify the sense of certain verbs by adding that of removal, loss, parting with, etc.; as, to throw away; to trifle away; to squander away, etc. Sometimes it has merely an intensive force; as, to blaze away.
